summary.intent: User wanted to activate all 7 hands in the OpenFang dashboard and fix the UI to properly display active hand instances.
summary.outcome: Successfully activated all 7 hands via API, installed missing dependencies (yt-dlp, python3), fixed dashboard to show active badges and load active instances on init, and rebuilt the binary. However, activations were lost on daemon restart since they're stored in-memory.

repo: OpenFang serves static files embedded at compile time via include_str!() - edits to HTML/JS require rebuilding the binary
Hand activations are stored in-memory and reset on daemon restart
Dashboard uses Alpine.js with separate loadData() for available hands and loadActive() for running instances
API runs on port 4200, dashboard/UI served on port 50051
Browser Hand dependency check looks for 'python' but macOS has 'python3', causing false negatives

code.finding: Hands page x-init only called loadData() (available hands) but never loadActive(), so active instances weren't fetched until user clicked the Active tab
loadActive() is only triggered by clicking the Active tab, not on page init

workflow: When static files are compiled into Rust binaries, the running daemon serves stale content until rebuilt and restarted
curl activations don't trigger client-side state updates in Alpine.js apps - need API responses or page refresh to see changes

friction: Static files embedded at compile time meant multiple rebuild cycles to test UI changes
Hand activations lost on daemon restart due to in-memory storage
Browser Hand dependency check incompatible with macOS python3 naming
open_items: Fix Browser Hand dependency check to look for 'python3' instead of 'python'
Twitter Hand needs TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN configured to actually post
Consider persisting hand activations across daemon restarts
Re-activate all hands after restart since they were lost
Verify dashboard now shows active badges and counts correctly after rebuild
